content.content:
1 # Desktop Settings Floating Panel Implementation Plan 2 3 ## Overview 4 5 Replace the in-renderer `#/settings` route inside the primary window with a dedicated, fixed-size floating settings window — the canonical macOS Big Sur preferences pattern (Safari, Mail, System Settings, Raycast, Linear desktop, Dia browser). The new window has horizontal icon-tab navigation across the top, four panes (Account, General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts), and is built in React inside the existing renderer bundle, gated by `data-window-kind="settings"`. The current `secondary` window kind — dead-code scaffolding labelled "for settings/about flows" but unused — is repurposed into the settings kind. 6 7 ## Current State Analysis 8 9 ### Multi-window infra is already in place 10 11 `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:90-99` already routes `BrowserWindowConstructorOptions` per `WindowKind` (`primary | secondary | hud`). Each kind has its own size, traffic-light position, and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. Adding/swapping a kind is a 1-line switch case plus shared/ipc.ts type update. 12 13 ### The renderer is single-bundle, hash-routed by kind 14 15 `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:101-110` loads `${devServerUrl}#${kind}` (or the file equivalent) so all windows share `index.html`. Preload exposes `codexWindowType` from `--window-kind=` argv (`apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts:95-99`). CSS at `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css:300-312` gates which `[data-kind-*]` section is visible per `:root[data-window-kind="…"]`. So a new window kind only needs (a) factory entry, (b) optional `[data-kind-settings]` section, (c) CSS gate. 16 17 ### Settings is currently a hash route inside the primary window 18 19 - Vanilla DOM rendered in `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221` — three sections (General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts). 20 - HTML anchor: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html:151-155` (`
`). 21 - Hash router: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts:1-13` parses `#/settings`. 22 - Entry points → all converge on `router.navigate("settings")`: 23 - Sidebar Settings button → `data-route-to="settings"` (`index.html:101-116`) 24 - User-menu dropdown → `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` (`react/user-menu.tsx:38`) 25 - Cmd+, hotkey → `dispatchAction("settings")` (`renderer/src/main.ts:121-123`) 26 - macOS App menu *Preferences* → `dispatchItem("settings", …)` → renderer `onMenuAction` → same dispatch (`menu.ts:70`, `main.ts:140`) 27 28 ### Settings store + IPC are already complete 29 30 `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts` is the source of truth (zod-validated `SettingsSnapshot`, listener-based change broadcast). `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:291-295` already broadcasts settings changes to **all** `BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()` — the new settings window will receive updates with no main-process changes. 31 32 ### `secondary` window kind is dead code 33 34 - Index.html copy literally describes its purpose: "Separate 720×540 window for settings/about flows" (`index.html:159`). 35 - Spawned by Cmd+N (`newThread` accelerator) and the demo "Open Secondary" button on the home page (`index.html:142-144`). 36 - Renderer shows a placeholder `

Secondary window

` section. 37 - No production flow uses it. 38 39 ### `account.get` tRPC procedure already exists 40 41 `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59` returns `{ id, firstName, lastName, fullName, username, primaryEmailAddress, imageUrl, createdAt }`. Already consumed by `react/user-menu.tsx:8` via `useTRPC().account.get.queryOptions()`. Re-use as-is for the Account pane. 42 43 ### Key Discoveries 44 45 - The `secondary` kind already uses `titleBarStyle: "hiddenInset"` with hidden traffic lights at `{16,16}` (`factory.ts:65-75`) — exactly the chrome the Dia/macOS-native settings window needs. 46 - `findWindow(kind)` in `main/index.ts:239-250` already gives single-instance focus-existing semantics for free. 47 - The renderer's `entry.tsx` mounts React via two roots (`#react-root` for AppShell, `#user-menu-root` for UserMenu) inside ``. Adding a third root (`#settings-root`) follows the same pattern. 48 - `attachLocalShortcuts` (`shortcuts.ts:5-27`) already wires Cmd+R reload + devtools per window — applies to settings window automatically since it's added in `openKind` (`main/index.ts:217-225`). 49 - Existing CSS reuse: `.switch`, `.segmented`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row` (styles.css:529-645) all transfer to React panes unchanged. `.settings-host` (220px sidebar grid at styles.css:463) and `.settings-nav` (vertical) **do not** transfer — the new chrome is horizontal-tab-bar across the top, requiring new classes. 50 51 ## Desired End State 52 53 After this plan: 54 55 1. Pressing **Cmd+,**, choosing **Lightfast → Settings…**, or selecting **Settings** in the sidebar's user-menu dropdown opens a fixed 720×640 floating window with native macOS hidden-inset traffic lights at `{16,16}` and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. 56 2. The window has a horizontal toolbar of four icon-tabs across the top: **Account**, **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts**. Selected pane content scrolls internally. 57 3. **Account** pane shows avatar, full name, primary email, and a Sign Out button — sourced from the existing `account.get` tRPC procedure. 58 4. **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts** panes match today's behavior (toggles for launchAtLogin / showInMenuBar / checkForUpdatesAutomatically; theme segmented control; read-only shortcut list). 59 5. Re-triggering the settings entry-point focuses the existing window rather than spawning a duplicate. 60 6. The window is **not resizable, not maximizable, not fullscreenable** (per Apple HIG and Rectangle issue #1353 — content-driven resize is the documented failure mode of this pattern). 61 7. The old `#/settings` route, `[data-route-settings]` section, vanilla `settings.ts` module, sidebar Settings nav button, `secondary` window kind, `newThread` accelerator, and the demo "Open Secondary" button are all removed. 62 63 ### Verification 64 65 - `cmd+,` opens a single 720×640 window with horizontal tab toolbar; pressing it again with the window already open just focuses it (not a second window). 66 - Toggling a setting (e.g. Launch at login) in the new window updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` and the change persists across app restart. 67 - Closing the settings window with red traffic light (or Cmd+W) keeps the primary window open and signed-in. 68 - `grep -r "#/settings\|data-route-settings\|secondary\|newThread\|renderSettings" apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits other than this plan reference. 69 70 ## What We're NOT Doing 71 72 - **No Sync pane.** Lightfast doesn't have desktop-sync today; mirroring Dia's Sync card would be aspirational scaffolding. 73 - **No Profiles pane.** Same reason. 74 - **No editable name/email.** Account pane is read-only mirror of Clerk profile + Sign out. Profile editing happens server-side in the web app. 75 - **No Updates pane.** Update auto-check toggle stays in General. Manual "Check now" button is out of scope. 76 - **No deep-linking** between panes via URL hash (e.g. `#account` selecting the Account tab from main process). React `useState` is sufficient; if needed later, a single IPC channel can reset selection. 77 - **No extraction of Cmd+N to mean "new primary window"** — `newThread` and the new-secondary menu entry are deleted outright (per user direction). 78 - **No native `NSWindowToolbar` integration.** Toolbar is plain HTML/CSS inside the renderer, matching how Dia itself implements it. 79 - **No reskin of the existing primary window**, sidebar, or user-menu UI beyond removing the now-dead Settings button. 80 81 ## Implementation Approach 82 83 Three sequential, independently shippable phases: 84 85 1. **Phase 1 — Plumbing only:** Rename the `secondary` kind to `settings`, reshape its window options, route Cmd+, / Preferences menu / user-menu Settings item to open it. After Phase 1, Cmd+, opens a fixed 720×640 window that renders the existing `home` page content (inert but harmless — the placeholder home page won't appear in a settings window anyway because we'll add the empty `[data-kind-settings]` section in this phase). The old in-renderer `#/settings` route still functions as a fallback (the user-menu and sidebar are repointed; only Cmd+, and Preferences menu now open the new window — wait, actually we change all three in Phase 1). 86 87 Actually, simplest: in Phase 1, **all** entry-points are repointed to the new window, and Phase 2 fills in the React UI. There is a brief "settings window opens but is mostly empty" state after Phase 1 — that's a deliberate phase-boundary checkpoint. 88 89 2. **Phase 2 — React UI inside the settings window:** Build `SettingsWindow.tsx` shell + four pane components. Mount in `entry.tsx` only when `codexWindowType === "settings"`. Reuse existing `.switch` / `.segmented` / `.settings-card` / `.settings-row` CSS for pane internals; add new `.settings-toolbar` / `.settings-toolbar__tab` classes for the horizontal-tab chrome. 90 91 3. **Phase 3 — Delete the old in-renderer settings surface:** Delete `settings.ts`, the route in `router.ts`, the `[data-route-settings]` HTML, the sidebar Settings button, settings-only CSS that's no longer referenced, and the `Route` type if `home` is now the only value (likely delete `router.ts` outright). 92 93 ## Execution Protocol 94 95 Phase boundaries halt execution. Automated checks passing is necessary but not sufficient — the next phase starts only on user go-ahead. 96 97 --- 98 99 ## Phase 1: Repurpose `secondary` window kind into `settings` 100 101 ### Overview 102 103 Pure plumbing rename + entry-point rewiring + dead-code removal. After this phase, Cmd+,, the *Lightfast → Settings…* menu, and the user-menu Settings item all open a single fixed-size 720×640 floating window. The new window currently renders a blank `[data-kind-settings]` section (filled in Phase 2). The old `#/settings` in-renderer route still works but is no longer reachable from any UI affordance — only by directly editing `window.location.hash`. It's deleted in Phase 3. 104 105 ### Changes Required 106 107 #### 1. `WindowKind` type 108 109 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/ipc.ts:46` 110 111 ```ts 112 // before 113 export type WindowKind = "primary" | "secondary" | "hud"; 114 115 // after 116 export type WindowKind = "primary" | "settings" | "hud"; 117 ``` 118 119 #### 2. Window factory — replace `secondaryOptions` with `settingsOptions` 120 121 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts` 122 123 ```ts 124 // replace secondaryOptions() at lines 65-75 125 function settingsOptions(): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { 126 const isMac = process.platform === "darwin"; 127 return { 128 ...baseWindowOptions(), 129 width: 720, 130 height: 640, 131 resizable: false, 132 maximizable: false, 133 fullscreenable: false, 134 minimizable: true, 135 ...(isMac && { trafficLightPosition: { x: 16, y: 16 } }), 136 }; 137 } 138 139 // switch case at lines 90-99 140 function optionsForKind(kind: WindowKind): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { 141 switch (kind) { 142 case "settings": 143 return settingsOptions(); 144 case "hud": 145 return hudOptions(); 146 default: 147 return primaryOptions(); 148 } 149 } 150 ``` 151 152 #### 3. Main process — rename `openSecondaryWindow` and rewire menu actions 153 154 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` 155 156 - Line 175: `if (kind === "secondary")` → `if (kind === "settings")`. 157 - Line 195: `type Kind = "primary" | "secondary" | "hud";` → `type Kind = "primary" | "settings" | "hud";`. 158 - Lines 231-233: `openSecondaryWindow()` → `openSettingsWindow()`. 159 - In `MenuActions` (passed at line 314-322), rename `openSecondary` → `openSettings`. 160 - Add a single-instance helper: when Cmd+, fires, focus existing settings window if any, else open one. 161 162 ```ts 163 function showSettingsWindow(): void { 164 const existing = findWindow("settings"); 165 if (existing) { 166 existing.show(); 167 existing.focus(); 168 return; 169 } 170 void openSettingsWindow(); 171 } 172 ``` 173 174 - Replace the `dispatchAction("settings")` flow. Currently the Preferences menu sends a `menuAction` to the focused window which calls `router.navigate("settings")`. New behavior: menu directly calls `showSettingsWindow`. Update `buildApplicationMenu` callsite at lines 314-322: 175 176 ```ts 177 Menu.setApplicationMenu( 178 buildApplicationMenu({ 179 openSettings: showSettingsWindow, 180 openHud: () => void openHudWindow(), 181 }) 182 ); 183 ``` 184 185 #### 4. Application menu — replace `openSecondary` and drop `newThread` 186 187 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/menu.ts` 188 189 - `MenuActions` interface (lines 52-55): 190 191 ```ts 192 export interface MenuActions { 193 openHud: () => void; 194 openSettings: () => void; 195 } 196 ``` 197 198 - App menu Preferences (line 70) — change from `dispatchItem("settings", ...)` to a direct click handler so the menu invocation doesn't depend on a focused renderer: 199 200 ```ts 201 { 202 label: t("app.preferences"), 203 accelerator: ACCELERATORS.settings, 204 click: actions.openSettings, 205 }, 206 ``` 207 208 - File menu (lines 82-104): 209 - Remove the `file.newSecondary` entry entirely (lines 85-89). 210 - Replace the non-Mac `dispatchItem("settings", t("file.settings"))` (line 99) with a direct-click variant matching the app-menu version. 211 212 #### 5. Accelerators — drop `newThread` 213 214 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/accelerators.ts:1-6` 215 216 ```ts 217 // before 218 export const ACCELERATORS = { 219 newThread: "CmdOrCtrl+N", 220 newWindow: "CmdOrCtrl+Shift+N", 221 settings: "CmdOrCtrl+,", 222 toggleSidebar: "CmdOrCtrl+B", 223 } as const; 224 225 // after 226 export const ACCELERATORS = { 227 newWindow: "CmdOrCtrl+Shift+N", 228 settings: "CmdOrCtrl+,", 229 toggleSidebar: "CmdOrCtrl+B", 230 } as const; 231 ``` 232 233 The `AcceleratorName` union narrows automatically. All consumers iterating `Object.keys(ACCELERATORS)` (`renderer/src/main.ts:136-138`, `settings.ts:109`) will lose the `newThread` row in shortcuts UI for free. 234 235 #### 6. Renderer dispatch — remove `newThread` case, repoint `settings` 236 237 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/main.ts:116-133` 238 239 ```ts 240 function dispatchAction(name: AcceleratorName): void { 241 switch (name) { 242 case "toggleSidebar": 243 sidebar.toggle(); 244 break; 245 case "settings": 246 void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("settings"); 247 break; 248 case "newWindow": 249 void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("primary"); 250 break; 251 default: 252 break; 253 } 254 } 255 ``` 256 257 (`newThread` case removed; `settings` case opens the new window instead of `router.navigate`.) 258 259 #### 7. User-menu — open settings window instead of route navigation 266 267 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/user-menu.tsx:35-43` 268 269 ```tsx 270 void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("settings")} 273 > 274 275 Settings 276 277 ``` 278 279 #### 9. Index.html — add empty settings section, remove dead-code 280 281 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` 282 283 - Remove the entire `[data-kind-secondary]` section (lines 156-163). 284 - Remove the demo "Open Secondary" button (lines 142-144). 285 - Remove the `` clause and its surrounding text from the content-hint (lines 137-139). Result: hint reads `Press to toggle the sidebar, for settings.` 286 - Add an empty settings section (will be populated by React in Phase 2): 287 288 ```html 289

The persisted `secondary` key in `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` becomes orphaned data; `loadWindowState` will simply not look it up since `WindowKind` no longer includes it. Future writes will be keyed under `settings`. 331 332 ### Success Criteria 333 334 #### Automated Verification 335 336 - [x] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` 337 - [x] Linting passes: `pnpm check` (Biome via root) — modified files clean; 6 pre-existing lint errors on `user-menu.tsx` are unchanged from baseline. 338 - [x] `grep -rn '"secondary"\|newThread\|openSecondary\|newSecondary' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. 339 - [x] `grep -rn 'data-kind-secondary\|data-open-window="secondary"' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. 340 341 #### Human Review 342 343 - [ ] Run `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → press Cmd+, → expect a 720×640 window opens with hidden-inset traffic lights, vibrancy backing, sized exactly 720×640, **not resizable** (cursor stays as arrow on edges). 344 - [ ] Press Cmd+, again → expect the existing window focuses; no duplicate window opens. 345 - [ ] Open *Lightfast → Settings…* from the macOS menu bar → same window opens / focuses. 346 - [ ] In the primary window's sidebar, click the user-menu (gear icon at bottom) → Settings → same window opens / focuses. 347 - [ ] The home page no longer shows the "Open Secondary" button → it's gone. 348 - [ ] The home page hint reads `Press ⌃B to toggle the sidebar, ⌘, for settings.` (no Cmd+N reference). 349 - [ ] Cmd+N does nothing (no toast, no error, no window) — accelerator is unbound. 350 - [ ] In the *File* menu, the "New Window" entry under the New section is gone (only "New HUD" remains, plus the Mac default Close). 351 352 --- 353 354 ## Phase 2: Build the React settings window 355 356 ### Overview 357 358 Mount a React tree inside the new `[data-kind-settings]` section. Top-level `SettingsWindow.tsx` renders horizontal icon-tab toolbar + active pane below. Four panes ported from the existing vanilla DOM (with the new Account pane added). 659 } 660 661 .settings-toolbar__icon { 662 width: 20px; 663 height: 20px; 664 } 665 666 .settings-toolbar__label { 667 font-size: var(--text-xs); 668 } 669 670 .settings-pane { 671 flex: 1; 672 min-height: 0; 673 padding: calc(var(--spacing) * 5) calc(var(--spacing) * 6); 674 overflow-y: auto; 675 } 676 677 .settings-pane .settings-section { 678 max-width: none; 679 margin: 0 0 calc(var(--spacing) * 4); 680 } 681 682 /* Account pane */ 683 684 .account-identity { 685 display: flex; 686 gap: calc(var(--spacing) * 3); 687 align-items: center; 688 } 689 690 .account-avatar { 691 width: 40px; 692 height: 40px; 693 object-fit: cover; 694 background: var(--color-background-elevated-secondary); 695 border-radius: 50%; 696 } 697 698 .account-avatar--placeholder { 699 background: var(--color-background-elevated-primary-opaque); 700 } 701 702 .account-name { 703 font-size: var(--text-sm); 704 font-weight: 500; 705 color: var(--color-text-foreground); 706 } 707 708 .account-email { 709 font-size: var(--text-xs); 710 color: var(--color-text-foreground-tertiary); 711 } 712 713 .settings-button { 714 -webkit-app-region: no-drag; 715 display: inline-flex; 716 gap: calc(var(--spacing) * 1.5); 717 align-items: center; 718 padding: calc(var(--spacing) * 1) calc(var(--spacing) * 2.5); 719 font: inherit; 720 font-size: var(--text-sm); 721 color: var(--color-text-foreground); 722 appearance: none; 723 cursor: default; 724 background: var(--color-background-elevated-secondary); 725 border: 1px solid var(--color-border); 726 border-radius: 6px; 727 } 728 729 .settings-button--destructive { 730 color: #ef4444; 731 } 732 733 .settings-button--destructive:hover { 734 background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.08); 735 } 736 ``` 737 738 (Token names like `--color-accent-foreground` may not exist; fall back to `--color-text-foreground`. Verify against existing tokens in the file when implementing.) 739 740 ### Success Criteria 741 742 #### Automated Verification 743 744 - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` 745 - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` 746 - [ ] No new files reference the deleted `secondary` kind: `grep -rn '"secondary"' apps/desktop/src` is empty. 747 748 #### Human Review 749 750 - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → Cmd+, → settings window shows horizontal toolbar with 4 tabs (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts), each with an icon and a label below. 751 - [ ] **Account pane**: avatar (or placeholder circle if no imageUrl), full name, email all render. The Sign Out button signs the user out and the primary window flips to the signed-out shell. 752 - [ ] **General pane**: toggling "Launch at login" / "Show in menu bar" / "Check for updates automatically" updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` immediately (verify with `cat`); changes survive an app restart. 753 - [ ] **Appearance pane**: clicking System / Light / Dark immediately changes both the primary window and the settings window theme. 754 - [ ] **Shortcuts pane**: shows ⌘B (Toggle sidebar), ⌘, (Open settings), ⇧⌘N (New primary window). The newThread row is **not** present. 755 - [ ] Active tab has the accent-color background pill; inactive tabs are subtle. 756 - [ ] Pane content area scrolls internally when overflowing; window itself does not resize. 757 - [ ] Title-bar drag region (the empty area above the tabs) lets you drag the window; clicking a tab does not. 758 759 --- 760 761 ## Phase 3: Delete the old in-renderer settings surface 762 763 ### Overview 764 765 The new window is fully wired and used by every entry-point. This phase deletes the now-orphaned vanilla DOM settings page, the hash route, and the sidebar navigation button. Net change is ~150 lines removed. 766 767 ### Changes Required 768 769 #### 1. Delete `settings.ts` 770 771 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts` — delete entirely. 772 773 #### 2. Renderer main — drop settings rendering & route handling 774 775 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/main.ts` 776 777 - Remove `import { renderSettings } from "./settings";` (line 13). 778 - Remove the `if (route === "settings")` block (lines 68-74) inside `renderForRoute`. 779 - Decision point: `Route` type now has only `home`. Recommend deleting `router.ts` entirely and replacing the few callers with `data-route-to` removal: 780 - `renderForRoute` becomes a no-op for routing; only the sidebar `.active` class loop remains. Move that into a one-shot init. 781 - `router.onChange(renderForRoute)` removed. 782 - Hotkey `dispatchAction` already only opens the settings window for the `settings` case (Phase 1 change), so no router calls remain in dispatchAction. 783 784 If keeping `router.ts` simpler than deleting it (e.g. for a future `home | inbox`), it's acceptable — but `Route` becomes literally `"home"`, which is silly. 785 786 #### 3. Delete `router.ts` (recommended) 787 788 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts` — delete entirely. 789 790 Replace its uses in `main.ts` with direct DOM init (no hash routing, just the sidebar active-state). 791 792 #### 4. Index.html — remove settings route section + sidebar Settings button 793 794 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` 795 796 - Remove the `[data-route-settings]` section (lines 151-155). 797 - Remove the sidebar Settings button (lines 101-116, the gear icon button with `data-route-to="settings"`). 798 - Remove `data-route="..."` document-element attribute logic (was being applied by `router.ts:apply()`); the corresponding CSS gating block at styles.css:288-298 needs cleanup too. 799 800 #### 5. CSS — remove route-based gating + unused settings classes 801 802 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` 803 804 - Remove the route-gating block (lines 288-298) that referenced `data-route="home" | "settings"`. 805 - Remove `.settings-host` and `.settings-nav` / `.settings-nav__item` (lines 463-500) — these were the old vertical-sidebar layout, replaced by `.settings-toolbar` in Phase 2. 806 - Keep `.settings-section`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row`, `.switch`, `.segmented` — still used by the new React panes. 807 808 #### 6. User-menu — already updated in Phase 1 809 810 No further change. 811 812 ### Success Criteria 813 814 #### Automated Verification 815 816 - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` 817 - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` 818 - [ ] `grep -rn 'renderSettings\|#/settings\|data-route-settings\|data-route-to="settings"\|settings-host\|settings-nav' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. 819 - [ ] `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'settings.ts' -o -name 'router.ts'` returns zero results. 820 821 #### Human Review 822 823 - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → primary window sidebar no longer has a Settings nav button (only Home, Projects, Agents, Runs). 824 - [ ] Manually setting `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` in the primary's devtools console no longer renders the old settings page (the section is gone). 825 - [ ] Cmd+, still opens the new floating settings window (regression check). 826 - [ ] All four panes still function (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts) — regression check. 827 828 --- 829 830 ## Testing Strategy 831 832 ### Unit Tests 833 834 The desktop app has no test infrastructure today. Don't introduce one as part of this plan. Verification is through the typecheck + lint commands and the human-review checks above. 835 836 ### Manual integration scenarios 837 838 - **Cold start with no settings window**: launch app → only primary opens. Cmd+, → settings opens. 839 - **Re-open behavior**: settings open → Cmd+, again → focuses existing, no second window. 840 - **Cross-window settings broadcast**: with both primary and settings open, change theme to Dark in settings → primary's theme updates immediately (via existing `IpcChannels.settingsChanged` broadcast in main/index.ts:291-295). 841 - **Sign-out from settings**: open Account pane → click Sign out → primary flips to `SignedOutShell`; settings window stays open or closes (check existing auth-flow expectations). 842 - **Settings persistence**: toggle Launch at login → quit app → relaunch → `getSettings()` returns `launchAtLogin: true` and the toggle reflects it. 843 - **Window-state persistence**: drag settings window to a different position → close → reopen → window appears at last position. `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` should contain a `settings` key after Phase 1 lands. 844 845 ### Regression checks 846 847 - HUD window still opens via `CmdOrCtrl+Alt+H` and the menu's *File → New HUD*. 848 - `Cmd+Shift+N` (`newWindow`) still spawns a second primary window. 849 - The macOS App menu's *About Lightfast* still works. 850 - The sign-in loopback flow (`auth-flow.ts`) is unchanged — settings window has no impact on auth. 851 852 ## Performance Considerations 853 854 - The settings React tree is small and only mounts once when the window opens. No measurable perf concern. 855 - The Vite optimizeDeps list (`vite.renderer.config.ts:16-23`) already includes `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`, `lucide-react`, `@repo/app-trpc/*`, `superjson`, `sonner` — sufficient for the new panes; no changes needed. 856 - Settings window is created on demand (only when Cmd+, fires) — does not affect cold-start of the primary window. 857 858 ## Migration Notes 859 860 - **window-state.json**: existing key `secondary` becomes orphaned but harmless. No migration code needed; it's user data. 861 - **settings.json**: schema unchanged. No migration. 862 - **Users with the old `#/settings` URL bookmarked**: not applicable — the renderer hash is internal, not user-facing. 863 864 ## References 865 866 - Original conversation: ad-hoc redesign request inspired by Dia browser settings panel screenshot (2026-05-04). 867 - Architecture parallel: macOS `NSWindowToolbarStylePreference` (Big Sur+). Apple HIG: . 868 - Documented failure mode (content-driven resize): Rectangle GitHub issue . 869 - Current settings entry-point: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221`. 870 - Current window-kind precedent: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:65-99`. 871 - Settings store: `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts`. 872 - Account tRPC procedure: `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59`. 873 - Existing React mount pattern: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx`. 874 - Related plan: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md` (parallel desktop work — this redesign is independent but should not conflict). 875

# Desktop Settings Floating Panel Implementation Plan ## Overview Replace the in-renderer `#/settings` route inside the primary window with a dedicated, fixed-size floating settings window — the canonical macOS Big Sur preferences pattern (Safari, Mail, System Settings, Raycast, Linear desktop, Dia browser). The new window has horizontal icon-tab navigation across the top, four panes (Account, General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts), and is built in React inside the existing renderer bundle, gated by `data-window-kind="settings"`. The current `secondary` window kind — dead-code scaffolding labelled "for settings/about flows" but unused — is repurposed into the settings kind. ## Current State Analysis ### Multi-window infra is already in place `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:90-99` already routes `BrowserWindowConstructorOptions` per `WindowKind` (`primary | secondary | hud`). Each kind has its own size, traffic-light position, and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. Adding/swapping a kind is a 1-line switch case plus shared/ipc.ts type update. ### The renderer is single-bundle, hash-routed by kind `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:101-110` loads `${devServerUrl}#${kind}` (or the file equivalent) so all windows share `index.html`. Preload exposes `codexWindowType` from `--window-kind=` argv (`apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts:95-99`). CSS at `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css:300-312` gates which `[data-kind-*]` section is visible per `:root[data-window-kind="…"]`. So a new window kind only needs (a) factory entry, (b) optional `[data-kind-settings]` section, (c) CSS gate. ### Settings is currently a hash route inside the primary window - Vanilla DOM rendered in `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221` — three sections (General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts). - HTML anchor: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html:151-155` (`
`). - Hash router: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts:1-13` parses `#/settings`. - Entry points → all converge on `router.navigate("settings")`: - Sidebar Settings button → `data-route-to="settings"` (`index.html:101-116`) - User-menu dropdown → `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` (`react/user-menu.tsx:38`) - Cmd+, hotkey → `dispatchAction("settings")` (`renderer/src/main.ts:121-123`) - macOS App menu *Preferences* → `dispatchItem("settings", …)` → renderer `onMenuAction` → same dispatch (`menu.ts:70`, `main.ts:140`) ### Settings store + IPC are already complete `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts` is the source of truth (zod-validated `SettingsSnapshot`, listener-based change broadcast). `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:291-295` already broadcasts settings changes to **all** `BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()` — the new settings window will receive updates with no main-process changes. ### `secondary` window kind is dead code - Index.html copy literally describes its purpose: "Separate 720×540 window for settings/about flows" (`index.html:159`). - Spawned by Cmd+N (`newThread` accelerator) and the demo "Open Secondary" button on the home page (`index.html:142-144`). - Renderer shows a placeholder `

Secondary window

` section. - No production flow uses it. ### `account.get` tRPC procedure already exists `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59` returns `{ id, firstName, lastName, fullName, username, primaryEmailAddress, imageUrl, createdAt }`. Already consumed by `react/user-menu.tsx:8` via `useTRPC().account.get.queryOptions()`. Re-use as-is for the Account pane. ### Key Discoveries - The `secondary` kind already uses `titleBarStyle: "hiddenInset"` with hidden traffic lights at `{16,16}` (`factory.ts:65-75`) — exactly the chrome the Dia/macOS-native settings window needs. - `findWindow(kind)` in `main/index.ts:239-250` already gives single-instance focus-existing semantics for free. - The renderer's `entry.tsx` mounts React via two roots (`#react-root` for AppShell, `#user-menu-root` for UserMenu) inside ``. Adding a third root (`#settings-root`) follows the same pattern. - `attachLocalShortcuts` (`shortcuts.ts:5-27`) already wires Cmd+R reload + devtools per window — applies to settings window automatically since it's added in `openKind` (`main/index.ts:217-225`). - Existing CSS reuse: `.switch`, `.segmented`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row` (styles.css:529-645) all transfer to React panes unchanged. `.settings-host` (220px sidebar grid at styles.css:463) and `.settings-nav` (vertical) **do not** transfer — the new chrome is horizontal-tab-bar across the top, requiring new classes. ## Desired End State After this plan: 1. Pressing **Cmd+,**, choosing **Lightfast → Settings…**, or selecting **Settings** in the sidebar's user-menu dropdown opens a fixed 720×640 floating window with native macOS hidden-inset traffic lights at `{16,16}` and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. 2. The window has a horizontal toolbar of four icon-tabs across the top: **Account**, **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts**. Selected pane content scrolls internally. 3. **Account** pane shows avatar, full name, primary email, and a Sign Out button — sourced from the existing `account.get` tRPC procedure. 4. **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts** panes match today's behavior (toggles for launchAtLogin / showInMenuBar / checkForUpdatesAutomatically; theme segmented control; read-only shortcut list). 5. Re-triggering the settings entry-point focuses the existing window rather than spawning a duplicate. 6. The window is **not resizable, not maximizable, not fullscreenable** (per Apple HIG and Rectangle issue #1353 — content-driven resize is the documented failure mode of this pattern). 7. The old `#/settings` route, `[data-route-settings]` section, vanilla `settings.ts` module, sidebar Settings nav button, `secondary` window kind, `newThread` accelerator, and the demo "Open Secondary" button are all removed. ### Verification - `cmd+,` opens a single 720×640 window with horizontal tab toolbar; pressing it again with the window already open just focuses it (not a second window). - Toggling a setting (e.g. Launch at login) in the new window updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` and the change persists across app restart. - Closing the settings window with red traffic light (or Cmd+W) keeps the primary window open and signed-in. - `grep -r "#/settings\|data-route-settings\|secondary\|newThread\|renderSettings" apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits other than this plan reference. ## What We're NOT Doing - **No Sync pane.** Lightfast doesn't have desktop-sync today; mirroring Dia's Sync card would be aspirational scaffolding. - **No Profiles pane.** Same reason. - **No editable name/email.** Account pane is read-only mirror of Clerk profile + Sign out. Profile editing happens server-side in the web app. - **No Updates pane.** Update auto-check toggle stays in General. Manual "Check now" button is out of scope. - **No deep-linking** between panes via URL hash (e.g. `#account` selecting the Account tab from main process). React `useState` is sufficient; if needed later, a single IPC channel can reset selection. - **No extraction of Cmd+N to mean "new primary window"** — `newThread` and the new-secondary menu entry are deleted outright (per user direction). - **No native `NSWindowToolbar` integration.** Toolbar is plain HTML/CSS inside the renderer, matching how Dia itself implements it. - **No reskin of the existing primary window**, sidebar, or user-menu UI beyond removing the now-dead Settings button. ## Implementation Approach Three sequential, independently shippable phases: 1. **Phase 1 — Plumbing only:** Rename the `secondary` kind to `settings`, reshape its window options, route Cmd+, / Preferences menu / user-menu Settings item to open it. After Phase 1, Cmd+, opens a fixed 720×640 window that renders the existing `home` page content (inert but harmless — the placeholder home page won't appear in a settings window anyway because we'll add the empty `[data-kind-settings]` section in this phase). The old in-renderer `#/settings` route still functions as a fallback (the user-menu and sidebar are repointed; only Cmd+, and Preferences menu now open the new window — wait, actually we change all three in Phase 1). Actually, simplest: in Phase 1, **all** entry-points are repointed to the new window, and Phase 2 fills in the React UI. There is a brief "settings window opens but is mostly empty" state after Phase 1 — that's a deliberate phase-boundary checkpoint. 2. **Phase 2 — React UI inside the settings window:** Build `SettingsWindow.tsx` shell + four pane components. Mount in `entry.tsx` only when `codexWindowType === "settings"`. Reuse existing `.switch` / `.segmented` / `.settings-card` / `.settings-row` CSS for pane internals; add new `.settings-toolbar` / `.settings-toolbar__tab` classes for the horizontal-tab chrome. 3. **Phase 3 — Delete the old in-renderer settings surface:** Delete `settings.ts`, the route in `router.ts`, the `[data-route-settings]` HTML, the sidebar Settings button, settings-only CSS that's no longer referenced, and the `Route` type if `home` is now the only value (likely delete `router.ts` outright). ## Execution Protocol Phase boundaries halt execution. Automated checks passing is necessary but not sufficient — the next phase starts only on user go-ahead. --- ## Phase 1: Repurpose `secondary` window kind into `settings` ### Overview Pure plumbing rename + entry-point rewiring + dead-code removal. After this phase, Cmd+,, the *Lightfast → Settings…* menu, and the user-menu Settings item all open a single fixed-size 720×640 floating window. The new window currently renders a blank `[data-kind-settings]` section (filled in Phase 2). The old `#/settings` in-renderer route still works but is no longer reachable from any UI affordance — only by directly editing `window.location.hash`. It's deleted in Phase 3. ### Changes Required #### 1. `WindowKind` type **File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/ipc.ts:46` ```ts // before export type WindowKind = "primary" | "secondary" | "hud"; // after export type WindowKind = "primary" | "settings" | "hud"; ``` #### 2. Window factory — replace `secondaryOptions` with `settingsOptions` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts` ```ts // replace secondaryOptions() at lines 65-75 function settingsOptions(): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { const isMac = process.platform === "darwin"; return { ...baseWindowOptions(), width: 720, height: 640, resizable: false, maximizable: false, fullscreenable: false, minimizable: true, ...(isMac && { trafficLightPosition: { x: 16, y: 16 } }), }; } // switch case at lines 90-99 function optionsForKind(kind: WindowKind): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { switch (kind) { case "settings": return settingsOptions(); case "hud": return hudOptions(); default: return primaryOptions(); } } ``` #### 3. Main process — rename `openSecondaryWindow` and rewire menu actions **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` - Line 175: `if (kind === "secondary")` → `if (kind === "settings")`. - Line 195: `type Kind = "primary" | "secondary" | "hud";` → `type Kind = "primary" | "settings" | "hud";`. - Lines 231-233: `openSecondaryWindow()` → `openSettingsWindow()`. - In `MenuActions` (passed at line 314-322), rename `openSecondary` → `openSettings`. - Add a single-instance helper: when Cmd+, fires, focus existing settings window if any, else open one. ```ts function showSettingsWindow(): void { const existing = findWindow("settings"); if (existing) { existing.show(); existing.focus(); return; } void openSettingsWindow(); } ``` - Replace the `dispatchAction("settings")` flow. Currently the Preferences menu sends a `menuAction` to the focused window which calls `router.navigate("settings")`. New behavior: menu directly calls `showSettingsWindow`. Update `buildApplicationMenu` callsite at lines 314-322: ```ts Menu.setApplicationMenu( buildApplicationMenu({ openSettings: showSettingsWindow, openHud: () => void openHudWindow(), }) ); ``` #### 4. Application menu — replace `openSecondary` and drop `newThread` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/menu.ts` - `MenuActions` interface (lines 52-55): ```ts export interface MenuActions { openHud: () => void; openSettings: () => void; } ``` - App menu Preferences (line 70) — change from `dispatchItem("settings", ...)` to a direct click handler so the menu invocation doesn't depend on a focused renderer: ```ts { label: t("app.preferences"), accelerator: ACCELERATORS.settings, click: actions.openSettings, }, ``` - File menu (lines 82-104): - Remove the `file.newSecondary` entry entirely (lines 85-89). - Replace the non-Mac `dispatchItem("settings", t("file.settings"))` (line 99) with a direct-click variant matching the app-menu version. #### 5. Accelerators — drop `newThread` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/accelerators.ts:1-6` ```ts // before export const ACCELERATORS = { newThread: "CmdOrCtrl+N", newWindow: "CmdOrCtrl+Shift+N", settings: "CmdOrCtrl+,", toggleSidebar: "CmdOrCtrl+B", } as const; // after export const ACCELERATORS = { newWindow: "CmdOrCtrl+Shift+N", settings: "CmdOrCtrl+,", toggleSidebar: "CmdOrCtrl+B", } as const; ``` The `AcceleratorName` union narrows automatically. All consumers iterating `Object.keys(ACCELERATORS)` (`renderer/src/main.ts:136-138`, `settings.ts:109`) will lose the `newThread` row in shortcuts UI for free. #### 6. Renderer dispatch — remove `newThread` case, repoint `settings` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/main.ts:116-133` ```ts function dispatchAction(name: AcceleratorName): void { switch (name) { case "toggleSidebar": sidebar.toggle(); break; case "settings": void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("settings"); break; case "newWindow": void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("primary"); break; default: break; } } ``` (`newThread` case removed; `settings` case opens the new window instead of `router.navigate`.) #### 7. Locale strings **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/locales/en.json` Remove `"file.newSecondary": "New Window"` (now unreferenced after dropping the menu entry). #### 8. User-menu — open settings window instead of route navigation **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/user-menu.tsx:35-43` ```tsx void window.lightfastBridge.openWindow("settings")} > Settings ``` #### 9. Index.html — add empty settings section, remove dead-code **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` - Remove the entire `[data-kind-secondary]` section (lines 156-163). - Remove the demo "Open Secondary" button (lines 142-144). - Remove the `` clause and its surrounding text from the content-hint (lines 137-139). Result: hint reads `Press to toggle the sidebar, for settings.` - Add an empty settings section (will be populated by React in Phase 2): ```html
``` #### 10. CSS — gate settings-window visibility **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` Replace lines 300-312 (the `secondary | hud` gating block): ```css :root[data-window-kind="settings"] [data-kind-settings], :root[data-window-kind="hud"] [data-kind-hud] { display: revert; } :root[data-window-kind="primary"] [data-kind-settings], :root[data-window-kind="primary"] [data-kind-hud], :root[data-window-kind="settings"] [data-kind-primary], :root[data-window-kind="settings"] .sidebar, :root[data-window-kind="hud"] [data-kind-primary], :root[data-window-kind="hud"] .sidebar { display: none; } ``` Also add a base style for the new section: ```css .settings-window { display: none; height: 100vh; padding: 0; background: transparent; } ``` #### 11. window-state.json migration No code change required. The persisted `secondary` key in `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` becomes orphaned data; `loadWindowState` will simply not look it up since `WindowKind` no longer includes it. Future writes will be keyed under `settings`. ### Success Criteria #### Automated Verification - [x] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` - [x] Linting passes: `pnpm check` (Biome via root) — modified files clean; 6 pre-existing lint errors on `user-menu.tsx` are unchanged from baseline. - [x] `grep -rn '"secondary"\|newThread\|openSecondary\|newSecondary' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. - [x] `grep -rn 'data-kind-secondary\|data-open-window="secondary"' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. #### Human Review - [ ] Run `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → press Cmd+, → expect a 720×640 window opens with hidden-inset traffic lights, vibrancy backing, sized exactly 720×640, **not resizable** (cursor stays as arrow on edges). - [ ] Press Cmd+, again → expect the existing window focuses; no duplicate window opens. - [ ] Open *Lightfast → Settings…* from the macOS menu bar → same window opens / focuses. - [ ] In the primary window's sidebar, click the user-menu (gear icon at bottom) → Settings → same window opens / focuses. - [ ] The home page no longer shows the "Open Secondary" button → it's gone. - [ ] The home page hint reads `Press ⌃B to toggle the sidebar, ⌘, for settings.` (no Cmd+N reference). - [ ] Cmd+N does nothing (no toast, no error, no window) — accelerator is unbound. - [ ] In the *File* menu, the "New Window" entry under the New section is gone (only "New HUD" remains, plus the Mac default Close). --- ## Phase 2: Build the React settings window ### Overview Mount a React tree inside the new `[data-kind-settings]` section. Top-level `SettingsWindow.tsx` renders horizontal icon-tab toolbar + active pane below. Four panes ported from the existing vanilla DOM (with the new Account pane added). Reuse existing CSS tokens for switch / segmented / cards / rows; add new toolbar classes. ### Changes Required #### 1. New directory structure ``` apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/ ├── SettingsWindow.tsx # shell + tab state ├── use-settings-snapshot.ts # subscribes to bridge.onSettingsChanged └── panes/ ├── account.tsx ├── general.tsx ├── appearance.tsx └── shortcuts.tsx ``` #### 2. Settings hook **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/use-settings-snapshot.ts` ```ts import { useEffect, useState } from "react"; import type { SettingsSnapshot } from "../../../../shared/ipc"; export function useSettingsSnapshot(): SettingsSnapshot { const [snapshot, setSnapshot] = useState( () => window.lightfastBridge.settings, ); useEffect( () => window.lightfastBridge.onSettingsChanged(setSnapshot), [], ); return snapshot; } ``` #### 3. `SettingsWindow.tsx` — shell + horizontal toolbar **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/SettingsWindow.tsx` ```tsx import { useState } from "react"; import { Keyboard, Palette, Settings as Gear, User } from "lucide-react"; import type { FormatPlatform } from "../../../../shared/accelerators"; import { Account } from "./panes/account"; import { Appearance } from "./panes/appearance"; import { General } from "./panes/general"; import { Shortcuts } from "./panes/shortcuts"; type PaneId = "account" | "general" | "appearance" | "shortcuts"; const TABS: Array<{ id: PaneId; label: string; Icon: typeof User }> = [ { id: "account", label: "Account", Icon: User }, { id: "general", label: "General", Icon: Gear }, { id: "appearance", label: "Appearance", Icon: Palette }, { id: "shortcuts", label: "Shortcuts", Icon: Keyboard }, ]; export function SettingsWindow({ platform }: { platform: FormatPlatform }) { const [pane, setPane] = useState("account"); return (

); } ``` #### 4. Account pane **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/account.tsx` Reuse `useTRPC().account.get` (already wired in `user-menu.tsx`): ```tsx import { useTRPC } from "@repo/app-trpc/react"; import { useQuery } from "@tanstack/react-query"; import { LogOut } from "lucide-react"; export function Account() { const trpc = useTRPC(); const query = useQuery(trpc.account.get.queryOptions()); const data = query.data; return (

General / Appearance / Shortcuts panes **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/general.tsx` Direct port of `settings.ts:69-81` to JSX, using `useSettingsSnapshot`: ```tsx import { useSettingsSnapshot } from "../use-settings-snapshot"; const TOGGLES = [ { key: "launchAtLogin", label: "Launch at login" }, { key: "showInMenuBar", label: "Show in menu bar" }, { key: "checkForUpdatesAutomatically", label: "Check for updates automatically" }, ] as const; export function General() { const snapshot = useSettingsSnapshot(); return (
{TOGGLES.map(({ key, label }) => (

Mount in `entry.tsx` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx` ```tsx import { DesktopTRPCProvider } from "@repo/app-trpc/desktop"; import { StrictMode } from "react"; import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client"; import type { FormatPlatform } from "../../../shared/accelerators"; import { AppShell } from "./app-shell"; import { SettingsWindow } from "./settings/SettingsWindow"; import { UserMenu } from "./user-menu"; const appOrigin = window.lightfastBridge.appOrigin; const platform = window.lightfastBridge.platform; const formatPlatform: FormatPlatform = platform === "darwin" || platform === "linux" || platform === "win32" ? platform : "linux"; function Providers({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ); } if (window.codexWindowType === "settings") { const settingsContainer = document.getElementById("settings-root"); if (settingsContainer) { createRoot(settingsContainer).render( , ); } } else { const container = document.getElementById("react-root"); if (container) { createRoot(container).render( , ); } const userMenuContainer = document.getElementById("user-menu-root"); if (userMenuContainer) { createRoot(userMenuContainer).render( , ); } } ``` The settings window does **not** mount AppShell or UserMenu — it has its own panes. AppShell's auth-gate redirect is moot because the settings window is only ever opened from a signed-in primary window context. #### 7. Toolbar + account-pane CSS **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` (add at end) ```css /* Settings window — Phase 2 */ .settings-window__inner { display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 100vh; background: transparent; } .settings-toolbar { -webkit-app-region: drag; display: flex; gap: calc(var(--spacing) * 1); justify-content: center; align-items: flex-end; padding: 36px calc(var(--spacing) * 4) calc(var(--spacing) * 2); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-border); } .settings-toolbar__tab { -webkit-app-region: no-drag; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 4px; min-width: 72px; padding: calc(var(--spacing) * 1.5) calc(var(--spacing) * 2); font: inherit; font-size: var(--text-xs); color: var(--color-text-foreground-tertiary); appearance: none; cursor: default; background: transparent; border: 0; border-radius: 6px; } .settings-toolbar__tab:hover { color: var(--color-text-foreground-secondary); background: var(--color-background-button-tertiary-hover); } .settings-toolbar__tab.active { color: var(--color-accent-foreground, var(--color-text-foreground)); background: var(--color-accent); } .settings-toolbar__icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; } .settings-toolbar__label { font-size: var(--text-xs); } .settings-pane { flex: 1; min-height: 0; padding: calc(var(--spacing) * 5) calc(var(--spacing) * 6); overflow-y: auto; } .settings-pane .settings-section { max-width: none; margin: 0 0 calc(var(--spacing) * 4); } /* Account pane */ .account-identity { display: flex; gap: calc(var(--spacing) * 3); align-items: center; } .account-avatar { width: 40px; height: 40px; object-fit: cover; background: var(--color-background-elevated-secondary); border-radius: 50%; } .account-avatar--placeholder { background: var(--color-background-elevated-primary-opaque); } .account-name { font-size: var(--text-sm); font-weight: 500; color: var(--color-text-foreground); } .account-email { font-size: var(--text-xs); color: var(--color-text-foreground-tertiary); } .settings-button { -webkit-app-region: no-drag; display: inline-flex; gap: calc(var(--spacing) * 1.5); align-items: center; padding: calc(var(--spacing) * 1) calc(var(--spacing) * 2.5); font: inherit; font-size: var(--text-sm); color: var(--color-text-foreground); appearance: none; cursor: default; background: var(--color-background-elevated-secondary); border: 1px solid var(--color-border); border-radius: 6px; } .settings-button--destructive { color: #ef4444; } .settings-button--destructive:hover { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.08); } ``` (Token names like `--color-accent-foreground` may not exist; fall back to `--color-text-foreground`. Verify against existing tokens in the file when implementing.) ### Success Criteria #### Automated Verification - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` - [ ] No new files reference the deleted `secondary` kind: `grep -rn '"secondary"' apps/desktop/src` is empty. #### Human Review - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → Cmd+, → settings window shows horizontal toolbar with 4 tabs (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts), each with an icon and a label below. - [ ] **Account pane**: avatar (or placeholder circle if no imageUrl), full name, email all render. The Sign Out button signs the user out and the primary window flips to the signed-out shell. - [ ] **General pane**: toggling "Launch at login" / "Show in menu bar" / "Check for updates automatically" updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` immediately (verify with `cat`); changes survive an app restart. - [ ] **Appearance pane**: clicking System / Light / Dark immediately changes both the primary window and the settings window theme. - [ ] **Shortcuts pane**: shows ⌘B (Toggle sidebar), ⌘, (Open settings), ⇧⌘N (New primary window). The newThread row is **not** present. - [ ] Active tab has the accent-color background pill; inactive tabs are subtle. - [ ] Pane content area scrolls internally when overflowing; window itself does not resize. - [ ] Title-bar drag region (the empty area above the tabs) lets you drag the window; clicking a tab does not. --- ## Phase 3: Delete the old in-renderer settings surface ### Overview The new window is fully wired and used by every entry-point. This phase deletes the now-orphaned vanilla DOM settings page, the hash route, and the sidebar navigation button. Net change is ~150 lines removed. ### Changes Required #### 1. Delete `settings.ts` **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts` — delete entirely. #### 2. Renderer main — drop settings rendering & route handling **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/main.ts` - Remove `import { renderSettings } from "./settings";` (line 13). - Remove the `if (route === "settings")` block (lines 68-74) inside `renderForRoute`. - Decision point: `Route` type now has only `home`. Recommend deleting `router.ts` entirely and replacing the few callers with `data-route-to` removal: - `renderForRoute` becomes a no-op for routing; only the sidebar `.active` class loop remains. Move that into a one-shot init. - `router.onChange(renderForRoute)` removed. - Hotkey `dispatchAction` already only opens the settings window for the `settings` case (Phase 1 change), so no router calls remain in dispatchAction. If keeping `router.ts` simpler than deleting it (e.g. for a future `home | inbox`), it's acceptable — but `Route` becomes literally `"home"`, which is silly. #### 3. Delete `router.ts` (recommended) **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts` — delete entirely. Replace its uses in `main.ts` with direct DOM init (no hash routing, just the sidebar active-state). #### 4. Index.html — remove settings route section + sidebar Settings button **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` - Remove the `[data-route-settings]` section (lines 151-155). - Remove the sidebar Settings button (lines 101-116, the gear icon button with `data-route-to="settings"`). - Remove `data-route="..."` document-element attribute logic (was being applied by `router.ts:apply()`); the corresponding CSS gating block at styles.css:288-298 needs cleanup too. #### 5. CSS — remove route-based gating + unused settings classes **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` - Remove the route-gating block (lines 288-298) that referenced `data-route="home" | "settings"`. - Remove `.settings-host` and `.settings-nav` / `.settings-nav__item` (lines 463-500) — these were the old vertical-sidebar layout, replaced by `.settings-toolbar` in Phase 2. - Keep `.settings-section`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row`, `.switch`, `.segmented` — still used by the new React panes. #### 6. User-menu — already updated in Phase 1 No further change. ### Success Criteria #### Automated Verification - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` - [ ] `grep -rn 'renderSettings\|#/settings\|data-route-settings\|data-route-to="settings"\|settings-host\|settings-nav' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. - [ ] `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'settings.ts' -o -name 'router.ts'` returns zero results. #### Human Review - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → primary window sidebar no longer has a Settings nav button (only Home, Projects, Agents, Runs). - [ ] Manually setting `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` in the primary's devtools console no longer renders the old settings page (the section is gone). - [ ] Cmd+, still opens the new floating settings window (regression check). - [ ] All four panes still function (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts) — regression check. --- ## Testing Strategy ### Unit Tests The desktop app has no test infrastructure today. Don't introduce one as part of this plan. Verification is through the typecheck + lint commands and the human-review checks above. ### Manual integration scenarios - **Cold start with no settings window**: launch app → only primary opens. Cmd+, → settings opens. - **Re-open behavior**: settings open → Cmd+, again → focuses existing, no second window. - **Cross-window settings broadcast**: with both primary and settings open, change theme to Dark in settings → primary's theme updates immediately (via existing `IpcChannels.settingsChanged` broadcast in main/index.ts:291-295). - **Sign-out from settings**: open Account pane → click Sign out → primary flips to `SignedOutShell`; settings window stays open or closes (check existing auth-flow expectations). - **Settings persistence**: toggle Launch at login → quit app → relaunch → `getSettings()` returns `launchAtLogin: true` and the toggle reflects it. - **Window-state persistence**: drag settings window to a different position → close → reopen → window appears at last position. `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` should contain a `settings` key after Phase 1 lands. ### Regression checks - HUD window still opens via `CmdOrCtrl+Alt+H` and the menu's *File → New HUD*. - `Cmd+Shift+N` (`newWindow`) still spawns a second primary window. - The macOS App menu's *About Lightfast* still works. - The sign-in loopback flow (`auth-flow.ts`) is unchanged — settings window has no impact on auth. ## Performance Considerations - The settings React tree is small and only mounts once when the window opens. No measurable perf concern. - The Vite optimizeDeps list (`vite.renderer.config.ts:16-23`) already includes `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`, `lucide-react`, `@repo/app-trpc/*`, `superjson`, `sonner` — sufficient for the new panes; no changes needed. - Settings window is created on demand (only when Cmd+, fires) — does not affect cold-start of the primary window. ## Migration Notes - **window-state.json**: existing key `secondary` becomes orphaned but harmless. No migration code needed; it's user data. - **settings.json**: schema unchanged. No migration. - **Users with the old `#/settings` URL bookmarked**: not applicable — the renderer hash is internal, not user-facing. ## References - Original conversation: ad-hoc redesign request inspired by Dia browser settings panel screenshot (2026-05-04). - Architecture parallel: macOS `NSWindowToolbarStylePreference` (Big Sur+). Apple HIG: . - Documented failure mode (content-driven resize): Rectangle GitHub issue . - Current settings entry-point: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221`. - Current window-kind precedent: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:65-99`. - Settings store: `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts`. - Account tRPC procedure: `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59`. - Existing React mount pattern: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx`. - Related plan: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md` (parallel desktop work — this redesign is independent but should not conflict).

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- foundation-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level foundation document for a company, product, or new primitive. This document captures thesis, mission, boundaries, actor model, surfaces, strategic bets, and open questions without collapsing ambiguity into implementation decisions. Applies when the user is still defining what the system is, what it is not, and what long-term direction it implies. Does NOT apply to concrete service specifications in `SPEC.md`, implementation plans, PRDs, or roadmap execution docs.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- spec-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level SPEC.md service specification — the language-agnostic behavioral spec that sits at a project's root and describes what a long-running service or automation does (problem statement, goals/non-goals, components, domain model). Applies when the user says things like "write a spec for X", "draft a specification", "update SPEC.md to add Y", "revise the non-goals", "add a new component to the spec", or hands over notes and asks for them to be turned into a formal service spec. Also applies when the user references an existing SPEC.md and wants edits, even if they don't explicitly use the word "spec". Covers both greenfield creation and targeted updates of an existing file. Does NOT apply to per-topic specs under thoughts/shared/specs/ (those are produced by create_ralph_topics from a JTBD), nor to JTBDs, implementation plans, or PRDs.
- lightfast-aeo: Use when authoring or reviewing any Lightfast content destined for AI engine retrieval —
release notes, blog posts, docs pages, integration pages, anything published to the
public site. Triggers on requests to write, draft, rewrite, or audit prose that should
be cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Codex, Google AI Overviews, or other answer engines,
even when the user doesn't say "AEO", "citations", or "answer engine". Per-content-type
skills compose this for voice and section ordering — load this one too whenever they
activate.
- lightfast-electron: Start, verify, and stop the Lightfast desktop Electron app from an agent
harness. Triggers when the user asks to start, launch, run, or boot the
desktop app, or when another skill (e.g. lightfast-desktop-signin) requires
the app to be running. Works around an upstream electron-forge bug where
`electron-forge start` exits ~7 ms after "Launched Electron app" when stdin
is non-TTY (the default for agent-spawned shells, CI, nohup, and
child_process.spawn). Humans on a real TTY should use `pnpm dev:desktop`
directly.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-debug: General-purpose Lightfast debugging workflow. Triggers when the user asks to debug,
investigate, trace, reproduce, inspect, or diagnose behavior across the app, platform,
Inngest workflows, database state, connected providers, or observability tooling. Use as
the broad entrypoint when no narrower skill is clearly better, when the failing surface is
unclear, or when a bug may cross multiple systems. Prefer `lightfast-db`,
`lightfast-inngest`, or `lightfast-sdk` for direct single-surface requests that clearly map
to one of those tools.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-clerk: Provision Clerk test users, sign in via the browser, mint JWTs, and tear
everything down — for local-dev tRPC / desktop / API testing. Triggers when
the user wants to call Lightfast tRPC procedures with a real auth token,
test desktop sign-in flows, or set up / clean up test users in Clerk dev.
Dev-only: refuses to run against pk_live_ keys or non-localhost URLs.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- resume_handoff: Resume work from a handoff document with context analysis and validation
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context
- create_handoff: Create a concise handoff document for resuming work in another session
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Phase 2 Complete - Ready for Manual Verification **Automated verification passed:** - Typecheck: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` clean - Lint: new settings tree (`react/settings/**`, `entry.tsx`) clean; the 13 `useSortedProperties` findings in `styles.css` are pre-existing on `HEAD` (count is unchanged by this phase) - `grep '"secondary"' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits **Files created:** - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/use-settings-snapshot.ts` — bridge subscription hook - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/settings-window.tsx` — shell + horizontal tab toolbar (file kebab-cased to match repo convention) - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/account.tsx` - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/general.tsx` - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/appearance.tsx` - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/settings/panes/shortcuts.tsx` **Files modified:** - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx` — gate React mount on `codexWindowType`; mount `SettingsWindow` for `settings`, otherwise mount `AppShell` + `UserMenu`. - `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` — added `.settings-window__inner`, `.settings-toolbar`, `.settings-toolbar__tab`, `.settings-pane`, account-pane, and `.settings-button` styles. **Deviations from the plan worth flagging:** - `SettingsWindow.tsx` → `settings-window.tsx` (Biome `useFilenamingConvention` requires kebab-case; matches the rest of `react/`). - `
` → `
` (Biome a11y forbids interactive role on `nav`). - `` avatar → `
` with `background-image` (Biome's `noImgElement` is on via `ultracite/next` — pure-CSS avatar matches the placeholder branch and avoids the rule). - `
` → drop `role="group"` (Biome `useSemanticElements` rejects it; the role is purely cosmetic). - Class concatenation uses ternaries returning whole strings instead of template literals — Biome's `useSortedClasses` "safe fix" stripped the leading space inside template literals, so I used `pane === id ? "settings-toolbar__tab active" : "settings-toolbar__tab"` instead. **Please perform the manual verification steps from the plan:** - `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → Cmd+, → settings window shows horizontal toolbar with 4 tabs (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts), each with icon and label. - **Account pane**: avatar (or placeholder circle if no imageUrl), full name, email all render. Sign Out signs out and the primary window flips to the signed-out shell. - **General pane**: toggling "Launch at login" / "Show in menu bar" / "Check for updates automatically" updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` immediately and survives a restart. - **Appearance pane**: clicking System / Light / Dark immediately changes both the primary and settings window themes. - **Shortcuts pane**: shows ⌘B (Toggle sidebar), ⌘, (Open settings), ⇧⌘N (New primary window). No `newThread` row. - Active tab has the elevated background pill; inactive tabs are subtle. - Pane content area scrolls internally when overflowing; window itself does not resize. - Title-bar drag region (the empty area above the tabs) lets you drag the window; clicking a tab does not. Let me know when manual testing is complete so I can proceed to Phase 3.
