content.content:
1 # Desktop Settings Floating Panel Implementation Plan 2 3 ## Overview 4 5 Replace the in-renderer `#/settings` route inside the primary window with a dedicated, fixed-size floating settings window — the canonical macOS Big Sur preferences pattern (Safari, Mail, System Settings, Raycast, Linear desktop, Dia browser). The new window has horizontal icon-tab navigation across the top, four panes (Account, General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts), and is built in React inside the existing renderer bundle, gated by `data-window-kind="settings"`. The current `secondary` window kind — dead-code scaffolding labelled "for settings/about flows" but unused — is repurposed into the settings kind. 6 7 ## Current State Analysis 8 9 ### Multi-window infra is already in place 10 11 `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:90-99` already routes `BrowserWindowConstructorOptions` per `WindowKind` (`primary | secondary | hud`). Each kind has its own size, traffic-light position, and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. Adding/swapping a kind is a 1-line switch case plus shared/ipc.ts type update. 12 13 ### The renderer is single-bundle, hash-routed by kind 14 15 `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:101-110` loads `${devServerUrl}#${kind}` (or the file equivalent) so all windows share `index.html`. Preload exposes `codexWindowType` from `--window-kind=` argv (`apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts:95-99`). CSS at `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css:300-312` gates which `[data-kind-*]` section is visible per `:root[data-window-kind="…"]`. So a new window kind only needs (a) factory entry, (b) optional `[data-kind-settings]` section, (c) CSS gate. 16 17 ### Settings is currently a hash route inside the primary window 18 19 - Vanilla DOM rendered in `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221` — three sections (General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts). 20 - HTML anchor: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html:151-155` (`
`). 21 - Hash router: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts:1-13` parses `#/settings`. 22 - Entry points → all converge on `router.navigate("settings")`: 23 - Sidebar Settings button → `data-route-to="settings"` (`index.html:101-116`) 24 - User-menu dropdown → `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` (`react/user-menu.tsx:38`) 25 - Cmd+, hotkey → `dispatchAction("settings")` (`renderer/src/main.ts:121-123`) 26 - macOS App menu *Preferences* → `dispatchItem("settings", …)` → renderer `onMenuAction` → same dispatch (`menu.ts:70`, `main.ts:140`) 27 28 ### Settings store + IPC are already complete 29 30 `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts` is the source of truth (zod-validated `SettingsSnapshot`, listener-based change broadcast). `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:291-295` already broadcasts settings changes to **all** `BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()` — the new settings window will receive updates with no main-process changes. 31 32 ### `secondary` window kind is dead code 33 34 - Index.html copy literally describes its purpose: "Separate 720×540 window for settings/about flows" (`index.html:159`). 35 - Spawned by Cmd+N (`newThread` accelerator) and the demo "Open Secondary" button on the home page (`index.html:142-144`). 36 - Renderer shows a placeholder `

Secondary window

` section. 37 - No production flow uses it. 38 39 ### `account.get` tRPC procedure already exists 40 41 `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59` returns `{ id, firstName, lastName, fullName, username, primaryEmailAddress, imageUrl, createdAt }`. Already consumed by `react/user-menu.tsx:8` via `useTRPC().account.get.queryOptions()`. Re-use as-is for the Account pane. 42 43 ### Key Discoveries 44 45 - The `secondary` kind already uses `titleBarStyle: "hiddenInset"` with hidden traffic lights at `{16,16}` (`factory.ts:65-75`) — exactly the chrome the Dia/macOS-native settings window needs. 46 - `findWindow(kind)` in `main/index.ts:239-250` already gives single-instance focus-existing semantics for free. 47 - The renderer's `entry.tsx` mounts React via two roots (`#react-root` for AppShell, `#user-menu-root` for UserMenu) inside ``. Adding a third root (`#settings-root`) follows the same pattern. 48 - `attachLocalShortcuts` (`shortcuts.ts:5-27`) already wires Cmd+R reload + devtools per window — applies to settings window automatically since it's added in `openKind` (`main/index.ts:217-225`). 49 - Existing CSS reuse: `.switch`, `.segmented`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row` (styles.css:529-645) all transfer to React panes unchanged. `.settings-host` (220px sidebar grid at styles.css:463) and `.settings-nav` (vertical) **do not** transfer — the new chrome is horizontal-tab-bar across the top, requiring new classes. 50 51 ## Desired End State 52 53 After this plan: 54 55 1. Pressing **Cmd+,**, choosing **Lightfast → Settings…**, or selecting **Settings** in the sidebar's user-menu dropdown opens a fixed 720×640 floating window with native macOS hidden-inset traffic lights at `{16,16}` and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. 56 2. The window has a horizontal toolbar of four icon-tabs across the top: **Account**, **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts**. Selected pane content scrolls internally. 57 3. **Account** pane shows avatar, full name, primary email, and a Sign Out button — sourced from the existing `account.get` tRPC procedure. 58 4. **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts** panes match today's behavior (toggles for launchAtLogin / showInMenuBar / checkForUpdatesAutomatically; theme segmented control; read-only shortcut list). 59 5. Re-triggering the settings entry-point focuses the existing window rather than spawning a duplicate. 60 6. The window is **not resizable, not maximizable, not fullscreenable** (per Apple HIG and Rectangle issue #1353 — content-driven resize is the documented failure mode of this pattern). 61 7. The old `#/settings` route, `[data-route-settings]` section, vanilla `settings.ts` module, sidebar Settings nav button, `secondary` window kind, `newThread` accelerator, and the demo "Open Secondary" button are all removed. 62 63 ### Verification 64 65 - `cmd+,` opens a single 720×640 window with horizontal tab toolbar; pressing it again with the window already open just focuses it (not a second window). 66 - Toggling a setting (e.g. Launch at login) in the new window updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` and the change persists across app restart. 67 - Closing the settings window with red traffic light (or Cmd+W) keeps the primary window open and signed-in. 68 - `grep -r "#/settings\|data-route-settings\|secondary\|newThread\|renderSettings" apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits other than this plan reference. 69 70 ## What We're NOT Doing 71 72 - **No Sync pane.** Lightfast doesn't have desktop-sync today; mirroring Dia's Sync card would be aspirational scaffolding. 73 - **No Profiles pane.** Same reason. 74 - **No editable name/email.** Account pane is read-only mirror of Clerk profile + Sign out. Profile editing happens server-side in the web app. 75 - **No Updates pane.** Update auto-check toggle stays in General. Manual "Check now" button is out of scope. 76 - **No deep-linking** between panes via URL hash (e.g. `#account` selecting the Account tab from main process). React `useState` is sufficient; if needed later, a single IPC channel can reset selection. 77 - **No extraction of Cmd+N to mean "new primary window"** — `newThread` and the new-secondary menu entry are deleted outright (per user direction). 78 - **No native `NSWindowToolbar` integration.** Toolbar is plain HTML/CSS inside the renderer, matching how Dia itself implements it. 79 - **No reskin of the existing primary window**, sidebar, or user-menu UI beyond removing the now-dead Settings button. 80 81 ## Implementation Approach 82 83 Three sequential, independently shippable phases: 84 85 1. **Phase 1 — Plumbing only:** Rename the `secondary` kind to `settings`, reshape its window options, route Cmd+, / Preferences menu / user-menu Settings item to open it. After Phase 1, Cmd+, opens a fixed 720×640 window that renders the existing `home` page content (inert but harmless — the placeholder home page won't appear in a settings window anyway because we'll add the empty `[data-kind-settings]` section in this phase). The old in-renderer `#/settings` route still functions as a fallback (the user-menu and sidebar are repointed; only Cmd+, and Preferences menu now open the new window — wait, actually we change all three in Phase 1). 86 87 Actually, simplest: in Phase 1, **all** entry-points are repointed to the new window, and Phase 2 fills in the React UI. There is a brief "settings window opens but is mostly empty" state after Phase 1 — that's a deliberate phase-boundary checkpoint. 88 89 2. **Phase 2 — React UI inside the settings window:** Build `SettingsWindow.tsx` shell + four pane components. Mount in `entry.tsx` only when `codexWindowType === "settings"`. Reuse existing `.switch` / `.segmented` / `.settings-card` / `.settings-row` CSS for pane internals; add new `.settings-toolbar` / `.settings-toolbar__tab` classes for the horizontal-tab chrome. 90 91 3. **Phase 3 — Delete the old in-renderer settings surface:** Delete `settings.ts`, the route in `router.ts`, the `[data-route-settings]` HTML, the sidebar Settings button, settings-only CSS that's no longer referenced, and the `Route` type if `home` is now the only value (likely delete `router.ts` outright). 92 93 ## Execution Protocol 94 95 Phase boundaries halt execution. Automated checks passing is necessary but not sufficient — the next phase starts only on user go-ahead. 96 97 --- 98 99 ## Phase 1: Repurpose `secondary` window kind into `settings` 100 101 ### Overview 102 103 Pure plumbing rename + entry-point rewiring + dead-code removal. After this phase, Cmd+,, the *Lightfast → Settings…* menu, and the user-menu Settings item all open a single fixed-size 720×640 floating window. The new window currently renders a blank `[data-kind-settings]` section (filled in Phase 2). The old `#/settings` in-renderer route still works but is no longer reachable from any UI affordance — only by directly editing `window.location.hash`. It's deleted in Phase 3. 104 105 ### Changes Required 106 107 #### 1. `WindowKind` type 108 109 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/ipc.ts:46` 110 111 ```ts 112 // before 113 export type WindowKind = "primary" | "secondary" | "hud"; 114 115 // after 116 export type WindowKind = "primary" | "settings" | "hud"; 117 ``` 118 119 #### 2. Window factory — replace `secondaryOptions` with `settingsOptions` 120 121 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts` 122 123 ```ts 124 // replace secondaryOptions() at lines 65-75 125 function settingsOptions(): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { 126 const isMac = process.platform === "darwin"; 127 return { 128 ...baseWindowOptions(), 129 width: 720, 130 height: 640, 131 resizable: false, 132 maximizable: false, 133 fullscreenable: false, 134 minimizable: true, 135 ...(isMac && { trafficLightPosition: { x: 16, y: 16 } }), 136 }; 137 } 138 139 // switch case at lines 90-99 140 function optionsForKind(kind: WindowKind): BrowserWindowConstructorOptions { 141 switch (kind) { 142 case "settings": 143 return settingsOptions(); 144 case "hud": 145 return hudOptions(); 146 default: 147 return primaryOptions(); 148 } 149 } 150 ``` 151 152 #### 3. Delete `router.ts` (recommended) 787 788 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts` — delete entirely. 789 790 Replace its uses in `main.ts` with direct DOM init (no hash routing, just the sidebar active-state). 791 792 #### 4. Index.html — remove settings route section + sidebar Settings button 793 794 **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` 795 796 - Remove the `[data-route-settings]` section (lines 151-155). 797 - Remove the sidebar Settings button (lines 101-116, the gear icon button with `data-route-to="settings"`). 798 - Remove `data-route="..."` document-element attribute logic (was being applied by `router.ts:apply()`); the corresponding CSS gating block at styles.css:288-298 needs cleanup too. 799 800 #### 5. User-menu — already updated in Phase 1 809 810 No further change. 811 812 ### Success Criteria 813 814 #### Automated Verification 815 816 - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` 817 - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` 818 - [ ] `grep -rn 'renderSettings\|#/settings\|data-route-settings\|data-route-to="settings"\|settings-host\|settings-nav' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. 819 - [ ] `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'settings.ts' -o -name 'router.ts'` returns zero results. 820 821 #### Human Review 822 823 - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → primary window sidebar no longer has a Settings nav button (only Home, Projects, Agents, Runs). 824 - [ ] Manually setting `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` in the primary's devtools console no longer renders the old settings page (the section is gone). 825 - [ ] Cmd+, still opens the new floating settings window (regression check). 826 - [ ] All four panes still function (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts) — regression check. 827 828 --- 829 830 ## Testing Strategy 831 832 ### Unit Tests 833 834 The desktop app has no test infrastructure today. Don't introduce one as part of this plan. Verification is through the typecheck + lint commands and the human-review checks above. 835 836 ### Manual integration scenarios 837 838 - **Cold start with no settings window**: launch app → only primary opens. Cmd+, → settings opens. 839 - **Re-open behavior**: settings open → Cmd+, again → focuses existing, no second window. 840 - **Cross-window settings broadcast**: with both primary and settings open, change theme to Dark in settings → primary's theme updates immediately (via existing `IpcChannels.settingsChanged` broadcast in main/index.ts:291-295). 841 - **Sign-out from settings**: open Account pane → click Sign out → primary flips to `SignedOutShell`; settings window stays open or closes (check existing auth-flow expectations). 842 - **Settings persistence**: toggle Launch at login → quit app → relaunch → `getSettings()` returns `launchAtLogin: true` and the toggle reflects it. 843 - **Window-state persistence**: drag settings window to a different position → close → reopen → window appears at last position. `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` should contain a `settings` key after Phase 1 lands. 844 845 ### Regression checks 846 847 - HUD window still opens via `CmdOrCtrl+Alt+H` and the menu's *File → New HUD*. 848 - `Cmd+Shift+N` (`newWindow`) still spawns a second primary window. 849 - The macOS App menu's *About Lightfast* still works. 850 - The sign-in loopback flow (`auth-flow.ts`) is unchanged — settings window has no impact on auth. 851 852 ## Performance Considerations 853 854 - The settings React tree is small and only mounts once when the window opens. No measurable perf concern. 855 - The Vite optimizeDeps list (`vite.renderer.config.ts:16-23`) already includes `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`, `lucide-react`, `@repo/app-trpc/*`, `superjson`, `sonner` — sufficient for the new panes; no changes needed. 856 - Settings window is created on demand (only when Cmd+, fires) — does not affect cold-start of the primary window. 857 858 ## Migration Notes 859 860 - **window-state.json**: existing key `secondary` becomes orphaned but harmless. No migration code needed; it's user data. 861 - **settings.json**: schema unchanged. No migration. 862 - **Users with the old `#/settings` URL bookmarked**: not applicable — the renderer hash is internal, not user-facing. 863 864 ## References 865 866 - Original conversation: ad-hoc redesign request inspired by Dia browser settings panel screenshot (2026-05-04). 867 - Architecture parallel: macOS `NSWindowToolbarStylePreference` (Big Sur+). Apple HIG: . 868 - Documented failure mode (content-driven resize): Rectangle GitHub issue . 869 - Current settings entry-point: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221`. 870 - Current window-kind precedent: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:65-99`. 871 - Settings store: `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts`. 872 - Account tRPC procedure: `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59`. 873 - Existing React mount pattern: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx`. 874 - Related plan: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md` (parallel desktop work — this redesign is independent but should not conflict). 875

# Desktop Settings Floating Panel Implementation Plan ## Overview Replace the in-renderer `#/settings` route inside the primary window with a dedicated, fixed-size floating settings window — the canonical macOS Big Sur preferences pattern (Safari, Mail, System Settings, Raycast, Linear desktop, Dia browser). The new window has horizontal icon-tab navigation across the top, four panes (Account, General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts), and is built in React inside the existing renderer bundle, gated by `data-window-kind="settings"`. The current `secondary` window kind — dead-code scaffolding labelled "for settings/about flows" but unused — is repurposed into the settings kind. ## Current State Analysis ### Multi-window infra is already in place `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:90-99` already routes `BrowserWindowConstructorOptions` per `WindowKind` (`primary | secondary | hud`). Each kind has its own size, traffic-light position, and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. Adding/swapping a kind is a 1-line switch case plus shared/ipc.ts type update. ### The renderer is single-bundle, hash-routed by kind `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:101-110` loads `${devServerUrl}#${kind}` (or the file equivalent) so all windows share `index.html`. Preload exposes `codexWindowType` from `--window-kind=` argv (`apps/desktop/src/preload/preload.ts:95-99`). CSS at `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css:300-312` gates which `[data-kind-*]` section is visible per `:root[data-window-kind="…"]`. So a new window kind only needs (a) factory entry, (b) optional `[data-kind-settings]` section, (c) CSS gate. ### Settings is currently a hash route inside the primary window - Vanilla DOM rendered in `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221` — three sections (General, Appearance, Keyboard Shortcuts). - HTML anchor: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html:151-155` (`
`). - Hash router: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts:1-13` parses `#/settings`. - Entry points → all converge on `router.navigate("settings")`: - Sidebar Settings button → `data-route-to="settings"` (`index.html:101-116`) - User-menu dropdown → `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` (`react/user-menu.tsx:38`) - Cmd+, hotkey → `dispatchAction("settings")` (`renderer/src/main.ts:121-123`) - macOS App menu *Preferences* → `dispatchItem("settings", …)` → renderer `onMenuAction` → same dispatch (`menu.ts:70`, `main.ts:140`) ### Settings store + IPC are already complete `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts` is the source of truth (zod-validated `SettingsSnapshot`, listener-based change broadcast). `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts:291-295` already broadcasts settings changes to **all** `BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()` — the new settings window will receive updates with no main-process changes. ### `secondary` window kind is dead code - Index.html copy literally describes its purpose: "Separate 720×540 window for settings/about flows" (`index.html:159`). - Spawned by Cmd+N (`newThread` accelerator) and the demo "Open Secondary" button on the home page (`index.html:142-144`). - Renderer shows a placeholder `

Secondary window

` section. - No production flow uses it. ### `account.get` tRPC procedure already exists `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59` returns `{ id, firstName, lastName, fullName, username, primaryEmailAddress, imageUrl, createdAt }`. Already consumed by `react/user-menu.tsx:8` via `useTRPC().account.get.queryOptions()`. Re-use as-is for the Account pane. ### Key Discoveries - The `secondary` kind already uses `titleBarStyle: "hiddenInset"` with hidden traffic lights at `{16,16}` (`factory.ts:65-75`) — exactly the chrome the Dia/macOS-native settings window needs. - `findWindow(kind)` in `main/index.ts:239-250` already gives single-instance focus-existing semantics for free. - The renderer's `entry.tsx` mounts React via two roots (`#react-root` for AppShell, `#user-menu-root` for UserMenu) inside ``. Adding a third root (`#settings-root`) follows the same pattern. - `attachLocalShortcuts` (`shortcuts.ts:5-27`) already wires Cmd+R reload + devtools per window — applies to settings window automatically since it's added in `openKind` (`main/index.ts:217-225`). - Existing CSS reuse: `.switch`, `.segmented`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row` (styles.css:529-645) all transfer to React panes unchanged. `.settings-host` (220px sidebar grid at styles.css:463) and `.settings-nav` (vertical) **do not** transfer — the new chrome is horizontal-tab-bar across the top, requiring new classes. ## Desired End State After this plan: 1. Pressing **Cmd+,**, choosing **Lightfast → Settings…**, or selecting **Settings** in the sidebar's user-menu dropdown opens a fixed 720×640 floating window with native macOS hidden-inset traffic lights at `{16,16}` and `vibrancy: "menu"` backing. 2. The window has a horizontal toolbar of four icon-tabs across the top: **Account**, **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts**. Selected pane content scrolls internally. 3. **Account** pane shows avatar, full name, primary email, and a Sign Out button — sourced from the existing `account.get` tRPC procedure. 4. **General**, **Appearance**, **Keyboard Shortcuts** panes match today's behavior (toggles for launchAtLogin / showInMenuBar / checkForUpdatesAutomatically; theme segmented control; read-only shortcut list). 5. Re-triggering the settings entry-point focuses the existing window rather than spawning a duplicate. 6. The window is **not resizable, not maximizable, not fullscreenable** (per Apple HIG and Rectangle issue #1353 — content-driven resize is the documented failure mode of this pattern). 7. The old `#/settings` route, `[data-route-settings]` section, vanilla `settings.ts` module, sidebar Settings nav button, `secondary` window kind, `newThread` accelerator, and the demo "Open Secondary" button are all removed. ### Verification - `cmd+,` opens a single 720×640 window with horizontal tab toolbar; pressing it again with the window already open just focuses it (not a second window). - Toggling a setting (e.g. Launch at login) in the new window updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/settings.json` and the change persists across app restart. - Closing the settings window with red traffic light (or Cmd+W) keeps the primary window open and signed-in. - `grep -r "#/settings\|data-route-settings\|secondary\|newThread\|renderSettings" apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits other than this plan reference. ## What We're NOT Doing - **No Sync pane.** Lightfast doesn't have desktop-sync today; mirroring Dia's Sync card would be aspirational scaffolding. - **No Profiles pane.** Same reason. - **No editable name/email.** Account pane is read-only mirror of Clerk profile + Sign out. Profile editing happens server-side in the web app. - **No Updates pane.** Update auto-check toggle stays in General. Manual "Check now" button is out of scope. - **No deep-linking** between panes via URL hash (e.g. `#account` selecting the Account tab from main process). React `useState` is sufficient; if needed later, a single IPC channel can reset selection. - **No extraction of Cmd+N to mean "new primary window"** — `newThread` and the new-secondary menu entry are deleted outright (per user direction). - **No native `NSWindowToolbar` integration.** Toolbar is plain HTML/CSS inside the renderer, matching how Dia itself implements it. - **No reskin of the existing primary window**, sidebar, or user-menu UI beyond removing the now-dead Settings button. ## Implementation Approach Three sequential, independently shippable phases: 1. **Phase 1 — Plumbing only:** Rename the `secondary` kind to `settings`, reshape its window options, route Cmd+, / Preferences menu / user-menu Settings item to open it. After Phase 1, Cmd+, opens a fixed 720×640 window that renders the existing `home` page content (inert but harmless — the placeholder home page won't appear in a settings window anyway because we'll add the empty `[data-kind-settings]` section in this phase). The old in-renderer `#/settings` route still functions as a fallback (the user-menu and sidebar are repointed; only Cmd+, and Preferences menu now open the new window — wait, actually we change all three in Phase 1). Actually, simplest: in Phase 1, **all** entry-points are repointed to the new window, and Phase 2 fills in the React UI. There is a brief "settings window opens but is mostly empty" state after Phase 1 — that's a deliberate phase-boundary checkpoint. 2. **Phase 2 — React UI inside the settings window:** Build `SettingsWindow.tsx` shell + four pane components. Mount in `entry.tsx` only when `codexWindowType === "settings"`. Reuse existing `.switch` / `.segmented` / `.settings-card` / `.settings-row` CSS for pane internals; add new `.settings-toolbar` / `.settings-toolbar__tab` classes for the horizontal-tab chrome. 3. **Phase 3 — Delete the old in-renderer settings surface:** Delete `settings.ts`, the route in `router.ts`, the `[data-route-settings]` HTML, the sidebar Settings button, settings-only CSS that's no longer referenced, and the `Route` type if `home` is now the only value (likely delete `router.ts` outright). ## Execution Protocol Phase boundaries halt execution. CSS — remove route-based gating + unused settings classes **File**: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` - Remove the route-gating block (lines 288-298) that referenced `data-route="home" | "settings"`. - Remove `.settings-host` and `.settings-nav` / `.settings-nav__item` (lines 463-500) — these were the old vertical-sidebar layout, replaced by `.settings-toolbar` in Phase 2. - Keep `.settings-section`, `.settings-card`, `.settings-row`, `.switch`, `.segmented` — still used by the new React panes. #### 6. User-menu — already updated in Phase 1 No further change. ### Success Criteria #### Automated Verification - [ ] Type checking passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` - [ ] Linting passes: `pnpm check` - [ ] `grep -rn 'renderSettings\|#/settings\|data-route-settings\|data-route-to="settings"\|settings-host\|settings-nav' apps/desktop/src` returns zero hits. - [ ] `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'settings.ts' -o -name 'router.ts'` returns zero results. #### Human Review - [ ] `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → primary window sidebar no longer has a Settings nav button (only Home, Projects, Agents, Runs). - [ ] Manually setting `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` in the primary's devtools console no longer renders the old settings page (the section is gone). - [ ] Cmd+, still opens the new floating settings window (regression check). - [ ] All four panes still function (Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts) — regression check. --- ## Testing Strategy ### Unit Tests The desktop app has no test infrastructure today. Don't introduce one as part of this plan. Verification is through the typecheck + lint commands and the human-review checks above. ### Manual integration scenarios - **Cold start with no settings window**: launch app → only primary opens. Cmd+, → settings opens. - **Re-open behavior**: settings open → Cmd+, again → focuses existing, no second window. - **Cross-window settings broadcast**: with both primary and settings open, change theme to Dark in settings → primary's theme updates immediately (via existing `IpcChannels.settingsChanged` broadcast in main/index.ts:291-295). - **Sign-out from settings**: open Account pane → click Sign out → primary flips to `SignedOutShell`; settings window stays open or closes (check existing auth-flow expectations). - **Settings persistence**: toggle Launch at login → quit app → relaunch → `getSettings()` returns `launchAtLogin: true` and the toggle reflects it. - **Window-state persistence**: drag settings window to a different position → close → reopen → window appears at last position. `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast/window-state.json` should contain a `settings` key after Phase 1 lands. ### Regression checks - HUD window still opens via `CmdOrCtrl+Alt+H` and the menu's *File → New HUD*. - `Cmd+Shift+N` (`newWindow`) still spawns a second primary window. - The macOS App menu's *About Lightfast* still works. - The sign-in loopback flow (`auth-flow.ts`) is unchanged — settings window has no impact on auth. ## Performance Considerations - The settings React tree is small and only mounts once when the window opens. No measurable perf concern. - The Vite optimizeDeps list (`vite.renderer.config.ts:16-23`) already includes `@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu`, `lucide-react`, `@repo/app-trpc/*`, `superjson`, `sonner` — sufficient for the new panes; no changes needed. - Settings window is created on demand (only when Cmd+, fires) — does not affect cold-start of the primary window. ## Migration Notes - **window-state.json**: existing key `secondary` becomes orphaned but harmless. No migration code needed; it's user data. - **settings.json**: schema unchanged. No migration. - **Users with the old `#/settings` URL bookmarked**: not applicable — the renderer hash is internal, not user-facing. ## References - Original conversation: ad-hoc redesign request inspired by Dia browser settings panel screenshot (2026-05-04). - Architecture parallel: macOS `NSWindowToolbarStylePreference` (Big Sur+). Apple HIG: . - Documented failure mode (content-driven resize): Rectangle GitHub issue . - Current settings entry-point: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts:122-221`. - Current window-kind precedent: `apps/desktop/src/main/windows/factory.ts:65-99`. - Settings store: `apps/desktop/src/main/settings-store.ts`. - Account tRPC procedure: `api/app/src/router/user/account.ts:29-59`. - Existing React mount pattern: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx`. - Related plan: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md` (parallel desktop work — this redesign is independent but should not conflict).

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- foundation-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level foundation document for a company, product, or new primitive. This document captures thesis, mission, boundaries, actor model, surfaces, strategic bets, and open questions without collapsing ambiguity into implementation decisions. Applies when the user is still defining what the system is, what it is not, and what long-term direction it implies. Does NOT apply to concrete service specifications in `SPEC.md`, implementation plans, PRDs, or roadmap execution docs.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- spec-creator: Use this skill when the user wants to write, draft, update, or revise a top-level SPEC.md service specification — the language-agnostic behavioral spec that sits at a project's root and describes what a long-running service or automation does (problem statement, goals/non-goals, components, domain model). Applies when the user says things like "write a spec for X", "draft a specification", "update SPEC.md to add Y", "revise the non-goals", "add a new component to the spec", or hands over notes and asks for them to be turned into a formal service spec. Also applies when the user references an existing SPEC.md and wants edits, even if they don't explicitly use the word "spec". Covers both greenfield creation and targeted updates of an existing file. Does NOT apply to per-topic specs under thoughts/shared/specs/ (those are produced by create_ralph_topics from a JTBD), nor to JTBDs, implementation plans, or PRDs.
- lightfast-aeo: Use when authoring or reviewing any Lightfast content destined for AI engine retrieval —
release notes, blog posts, docs pages, integration pages, anything published to the
public site. Triggers on requests to write, draft, rewrite, or audit prose that should
be cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Codex, Google AI Overviews, or other answer engines,
even when the user doesn't say "AEO", "citations", or "answer engine". Per-content-type
skills compose this for voice and section ordering — load this one too whenever they
activate.
- lightfast-electron: Start, verify, and stop the Lightfast desktop Electron app from an agent
harness. Triggers when the user asks to start, launch, run, or boot the
desktop app, or when another skill (e.g. lightfast-desktop-signin) requires
the app to be running. Works around an upstream electron-forge bug where
`electron-forge start` exits ~7 ms after "Launched Electron app" when stdin
is non-TTY (the default for agent-spawned shells, CI, nohup, and
child_process.spawn). Humans on a real TTY should use `pnpm dev:desktop`
directly.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-debug: General-purpose Lightfast debugging workflow. Triggers when the user asks to debug,
investigate, trace, reproduce, inspect, or diagnose behavior across the app, platform,
Inngest workflows, database state, connected providers, or observability tooling. Use as
the broad entrypoint when no narrower skill is clearly better, when the failing surface is
unclear, or when a bug may cross multiple systems. Prefer `lightfast-db`,
`lightfast-inngest`, or `lightfast-sdk` for direct single-surface requests that clearly map
to one of those tools.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-clerk: Provision Clerk test users, sign in via the browser, mint JWTs, and tear
everything down — for local-dev tRPC / desktop / API testing. Triggers when
the user wants to call Lightfast tRPC procedures with a real auth token,
test desktop sign-in flows, or set up / clean up test users in Clerk dev.
Dev-only: refuses to run against pk_live_ keys or non-localhost URLs.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- resume_handoff: Resume work from a handoff document with context analysis and validation
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context
- create_handoff: Create a concise handoff document for resuming work in another session
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
## Phase 3 Complete — Ready for Manual Verification

**Automated checks passed:**
- Typecheck: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` clean
- Lint: `main.ts`, `entry.tsx`, and `react/settings/**` clean. `styles.css` still has the 13 pre-existing `useSortedProperties` findings (unchanged). The `index.html` SVG-formatting warning is pre-existing on `HEAD` (verified by stash + biome).
- `grep -rn 'renderSettings|#/settings|data-route-settings|data-route-to="settings"|settings-host|settings-nav|.settings-root|data-route="' apps/desktop/src` → zero hits.
- `find apps/desktop/src/renderer/src -name 'settings.ts' -o -name 'router.ts'` → zero results.

**Files deleted:**
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/settings.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/router.ts`

**Files modified:**
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/main.ts` — dropped `./router` and `./settings` imports, removed `createRouter`, `renderForRoute`, `router.onChange`, and the `[data-route-to]` click handler loop.
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/index.html` — removed the sidebar Settings nav button, the entire `[data-route-settings]` section, the `data-route-home` attribute on the Home content, and the `data-route-to="home"` attribute on the Home sidebar item (since routing is gone).
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/styles.css` — replaced the route-gating block with kind-only gating (`:root[data-window-kind="primary"] .content-inner[data-kind-primary]`); deleted `.settings-root`, `.settings-host`, `.settings-nav`, `.settings-nav__item`, `.settings-nav__item:hover`, `.settings-nav__item.active`, `.settings-content`; updated `.content-inner:not(.settings-root)` → `.content-inner:not(.settings-window)` so the new floating settings window keeps its full-width chrome.

**Deviation worth flagging:**
- The plan suggests "remove `data-route="..."` document-element attribute logic; the corresponding CSS gating block at styles.css:288-298 needs cleanup". I did this by collapsing the two gating blocks (route-based + kind-based) into a single kind-only block. The new selector `.content-inner[data-kind-primary]` is more specific than the old `[data-kind-primary]` so it doesn't accidentally `display: revert` the title-bar or sidebar (whose flex/fixed display we want to keep).

**Please run the manual verification:**
- `cd apps/desktop && pnpm dev` → primary window sidebar shows only Home / Projects / Agents / Runs (no Settings nav button).
- In primary devtools console: `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` does **not** render the old settings page (the section is gone; primary content stays on home).
- Cmd+, still opens the floating settings window (regression check).
- All four settings panes still function — Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts (regression check).
- Theme switching, settings persistence, sign out all still work end-to-end.

Let me know once manual checks pass and I'll close out the plan.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/.claude/skills/lightfast-electron

# Lightfast Electron Skill

Agent-only runbook for getting the Electron desktop dev server (Electron +
Vite on `:5173`) up, verified, and torn down. The existing `pnpm dev:desktop`
script works fine from a TTY but exits within milliseconds when an agent
spawns it, because forge's CLI hardcodes `interactive: true` and calls
`process.stdin.resume()`. This skill encodes the verified workaround.

## When to use

You need the desktop dev server running. This includes: driving sign-in
via `lightfast-desktop-signin`, smoke-testing renderer surfaces, exercising
tRPC procedures from the desktop, or any end-to-end flow that requires a
live Electron window.

Humans on a real TTY: use `pnpm dev:desktop` directly. The interactive
`rs` restart keystroke still works there. The pipe trick below is purely
an agent workaround.

## Why this skill exists

`@electron-forge/cli@7.11.1` (latest as of 2026-04-26) hardcodes
`interactive: true` in `electron-forge-start.ts`, which calls
`process.stdin.resume()` and exits the moment stdin EOFs — the default
for non-TTY contexts. The `@electron-forge/plugin-vite` `process.on('exit')`
handler then tears down the spawned Electron child. Net effect: forge
dies in ~7 ms when an agent spawns it.

Verified upstream — same bug class fixed in
[vitejs/vite#11262](https://github.com/vitejs/vite) and
[slidevjs/slidev#2497](https://github.com/slidevjs/slidev/issues/2497)
with a `process.stdin.isTTY` guard. electron-forge's own `init` CLI
already uses this guard; only `start` was missed. No CLI flag, env var,
or newer version exists today.

## Start

```bash
tail -f /dev/null | pnpm dev:desktop > /tmp/lightfast-desktop.log 2>&1 &
```

The `tail -f /dev/null` keeps a non-EOF source piped into forge's stdin,
which prevents the `process.stdin.resume()` exit path. The pipe lives
inside the npm-script subshell, so the parent agent shell can background
it normally with `&` without disturbing the pipe.

## Verify it's up (give it ~10 s)

```bash
sleep 10
pgrep -f 'Electron\.app/Contents/MacOS/Electron' | head -1   # should print a PID
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://localhost:5173/   # should print 200
```

If either fails, tail the log: `tail -50 /tmp/lightfast-desktop.log`.

## Stop

```bash
pkill -f 'Electron\.app/Contents/MacOS/Electron'
pkill -f electron-forge
pkill -f 'tail -f /dev/null'   # only if you started one — be aware this
                                # matches any tail -f /dev/null on the box
```

## If a stale Singleton lock blocks startup

```bash
rm -f ~/Library/Application\ Support/Lightfast\ Dev/Singleton*
```

This happens when a previous Electron process was SIGKILLed. Chromium's
own logic usually clears stale-PID locks on next launch, but if the next
launch fails immediately, clearing manually is safe.

## Boundaries

- macOS only. Linux works the same; Windows desktop dev is out of scope.
- This is a workaround. The right fix is upstream: `electron-forge`
  should guard `process.stdin.resume()` with `process.stdin.isTTY`,
  matching the pattern already used in its own `init` CLI and in
  Slidev/Vite. Filing the upstream issue is tracked separately.
- This skill does not start the API mesh (`pnpm dev:app`,
  `pnpm dev:platform`). If your flow requires those, start them first;
  the desktop app expects `:3024` to be reachable.

content.text:
Phase 3 boots clean. Let me leave the app running for your visual inspection and summarize what I verified live vs what still needs your eyes.

**Live-verified:**
- Electron + Vite boot clean (PID 40310, http://localhost:5173 → 200)
- Renderer HTML matches Phase 3 cleanup — `curl http://localhost:5173/ | grep` shows only `data-kind-primary`, `data-kind-settings`, `data-kind-hud`. No `data-route-to`, no `data-route-settings`, no `data-route-home`, no `Settings` sidebar item label.
- Cmd+, opens the floating settings window (window count 2 → 4 with devtools).
- Cmd+, pressed again **focuses** the existing window — count stays at 4 (single-instance works).
- Zero runtime errors in the log after window operations (the only `ERROR:CONSOLE` lines are unrelated Chromium Autofill devtools chatter).

**Needs your eyes (the app is running — switch to it now):**
- Primary window sidebar shows **only** Home / Projects / Agents / Runs (no Settings nav button).
- Settings window has horizontal toolbar with 4 tabs: Account / General / Appearance / Shortcuts.
- Active tab pill styling looks right.
- Toggling General settings updates `~/Library/Application Support/Lightfast Dev/settings.json` and persists across restart.
- Theme segmented control in Appearance flips both windows.
- Shortcuts pane shows ⌘B / ⌘, / ⇧⌘N (no `newThread` row).
- Pane scrolls internally when content overflows.
- Drag region: empty area above tabs drags the window; clicking a tab does not.
- In primary devtools console, run `window.location.hash = "#/settings"` — should do nothing visible (route handling is gone; section was deleted).

**Caveat:** I started only `pnpm dev:desktop`, not the API mesh. The Account pane will show "—" / blank because `account.get` tRPC call has no backend. If you want to validate the Account pane rendering with real data + Sign Out, let me know and I'll spin up `pnpm dev:full` too.

The dev server is running in the background. To stop it: `pkill -f 'Electron\.app/Contents/MacOS/Electron'; pkill -f electron-forge; pkill -f 'tail -f /dev/null'`.

content.text:
# Commit Changes

You are tasked with creating git commits for the changes made during this session.

## Process:

1. **Think about what changed:**
   - Review the conversation history and understand what was accomplished
   - Run `git status` to see current changes
   - Run `git diff` to understand the modifications
   - Consider whether changes should be one commit or multiple logical commits

2. **Plan your commit(s):**
   - Identify which files belong together
   - Draft clear, descriptive commit messages
   - Use imperative mood in commit messages
   - Focus on why the changes were made, not just what

3. **Present your plan to the user:**
   - List the files you plan to add for each commit
   - Show the commit message(s) you'll use
   - Ask: "I plan to create [N] commit(s) with these changes. Shall I proceed?"

4. **Execute upon confirmation:**
   - Use `git add` with specific files (never use `-A` or `.`)
   - Create commits with your planned messages
   - Show the result with `git log --oneline -n [number]`

## Remember:

- You have the full context of what was done in this session
- Group related changes together
- Keep commits focused and atomic when possible
- The user trusts your judgment - they asked you to commit


ARGUMENTS: apps/desktop

content.text:
## Commit Plan

**One commit**, scoped to `apps/desktop` only (the plan file in `thoughts/` and other unrelated working-tree changes stay uncommitted).

**Files to stage** (13 modified + 2 deleted + 6 new):
- Modified: `src/main/index.ts`, `src/main/locales/en.json`, `src/main/menu.ts`, `src/main/windows/factory.ts`, `src/renderer/index.html`, `src/renderer/src/main.ts`, `src/renderer/src/react/entry.tsx`, `src/renderer/src/react/user-menu.tsx`, `src/renderer/src/styles.css`, `src/shared/accelerators.ts`, `src/shared/ipc.ts`
- Deleted: `src/renderer/src/router.ts`, `src/renderer/src/settings.ts`
- New: `src/renderer/src/react/settings/{settings-window.tsx, use-settings-snapshot.ts, panes/{account.tsx, general.tsx, appearance.tsx, shortcuts.tsx}}`

**Commit message:**
```
Replace in-renderer settings with floating window

Repurposes the unused `secondary` window kind into a fixed 720x640
floating window with horizontal toolbar tabs (Account / General /
Appearance / Shortcuts) — the macOS Big Sur preferences pattern. Cmd+,,
Lightfast > Settings…, and the user-menu Settings item all open it
with single-instance focus.

Deletes the old #/settings hash route, vanilla-DOM settings.ts,
router.ts, sidebar Settings button, [data-route-settings] section, and
the unused newThread accelerator + "Open Secondary" demo button.
```

I plan to create 1 commit with these changes. Shall I proceed?
